summary: Milestone 2 (inbound channels + centralized ingest_order pipeline) fully verified. Backend: 12/12 new pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_milestone2_channels.py pass in 10s. Covers: (1) WhatsApp webhook VERIFY handshake — correct verify_token returns plain challenge '12345' with 200, wrong token returns 403; (2) WhatsApp webhook RECEIVE text — Meta-style payload with entry->changes->value.metadata.phone_number_id + messages[0] type=text 'Ciao, 3 casse mozzarella e 2 sacchi farina' → {status:ok, orders_created:1}; the resulting order has source_type='whatsapp', source_meta.from='393401112233', and Claude matched a line to Mozzarella; (3) WhatsApp idempotency — reposting the same messages[0].id returns orders_created:0 and the whatsapp order count on the tenant is unchanged; (4) WhatsApp image branch with dummy access_token — Meta media resolve fails (401) and endpoint returns 200 with orders_created:0 (no 500, graceful); (5) Email poll gating — POST /api/integrations/email/poll before any email integration exists returns 400 with Italian detail; configuring 'forwarding' provider + validate → status connected; polling forwarding is a no-op (orders_created:0, no crash); configuring gmail IMAP with fake credentials → validate returns 400 with Italian error 'Autenticazione email fallita: ...' (NO 500); (6) Centralized pipeline regression — POST /api/orders/extract (source_type=text) still creates an order via ingest_order; POST /api/orders/{id}/validate persists status='validated' AND grows GET /api/learning; re-extracting the same phrase auto-matches with learned=true and confidence>=0.99; (7) Multi-tenant isolation — company_b (a fresh @example.com registration) sees zero whatsapp orders while company_a still sees its own. Frontend smoke via Playwright (pilot demo tenant, auto-login): pilot dashboard at '/' redirects to '/app' rendering 'Centro di Comando' + KPIs (10 orders, incl. one whatsapp-source 'Bar Centrale'); /app/setup/email renders Italian labels (Configurazione, Ricezione ordini, Invio email, providers Gmail/M365/Altro IMAP, 'Indirizzo di inoltro Ordia', forwarding address 'orders-4620d78f@inbound.ordia.eu' visible); the 'Controlla ora la posta' (email-poll-now) button is correctly HIDDEN because the demo tenant is in 'forwarding' provider mode — exactly per spec ('only appears when status connected + IMAP provider'); /app/setup/whatsapp wizard renders full 5-step flow (Prerequisiti/Credenziali/Verifica/Test/Fatto).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now >1200 lines — consider splitting into modules (auth, catalog, orders, integrations/whatsapp, integrations/email, integrations/erp, pilot_seed). Testability and diff review will improve significantly.

whatsapp_webhook_receive() does NOT verify Meta's X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC using app_secret. Any external actor who knows the phone_number_id can inject fake orders. Recommend rejecting requests without a valid signature when app_secret is configured. This is a security gap, not a functional one — flag as HIGH for production readiness.

_wa_download_media uses requests.get(url, headers=...) with the bearer token also on the CDN URL (Meta's lookaside CDN); this works but the second call should keep the Authorization header only if the URL is still on graph.facebook.com — otherwise leaks the token to third-party CDNs. Confirm URL host before re-sending Authorization.

email_poll_loop() starts on module import (via startup event presumably); a crashed IMAP loop will still be swallowed but there is no visibility (no metric / no `last_polled_at` per account in the response of GET /api/integrations/email). Add last_polled_at + last_created_count so operators can tell polling is alive.

ingest_order() uses external_id for idempotency but there is no unique Mongo index on (company_id, external_id). Under concurrent webhook retries the same message could still be inserted twice before the find_one check. Add a unique compound index (idempotency guard at DB layer).

poll_email_account swallows all IMAP errors into `last_error` but does not disable the account after N consecutive failures — a permanently broken mailbox will retry forever every EMAIL_POLL_INTERVAL seconds and log-spam. Add a failure-count → auto-pause with UI notification.

run_extraction() prompt is English-only ('You are Ordia's expert order-entry assistant...') even though inputs and outputs are Italian; Claude handles it but a system message in Italian would reduce token cost and improve edge cases with abbreviations like 'pz', 'cf', 'cs'.
test_credentials: Demo: demo@ordia.app / demo123. New per-run companies test_A_<hex>@example.com and test_B_<hex>@example.com registered via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 2 fresh companies (@example.com) via register, 1 WhatsApp integration on company_a with dummy Meta creds (kept, harmless — it is only reachable by webhooks bearing its phone_number_id), 1 email integration on each company (forwarding on B, gmail IMAP fake on A). Whatsapp orders on company_a persist across runs; harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_milestone2_channels.py is the regression suite for the inbound-channels milestone. It hits /api/webhooks/whatsapp WITHOUT auth headers (public endpoint, correct per Meta spec). No real Meta / IMAP creds needed: verify uses tokens generated by /api/integrations/whatsapp save; media download is expected to 401 against Meta and MUST return orders_created:0 (guard the graceful path). If Meta or graph.facebook.com becomes unreachable, only the image-branch test is affected and it will still pass (the wrapper catches the network exception). Learning-loop test depends on Claude via emergentintegrations — allow ~10s wall time. Previous iterations 1-3 suites (backend_test.py, test_pilot_and_voice.py, test_setup_integrations.py) still cover the earlier surface area and were not re-run this iteration.
rca of the issue: No bugs found this iteration. All Milestone 2 acceptance criteria — VERIFY handshake, RECEIVE text ingest, idempotency via external_id, graceful media-failure, email-poll gating (400 before connect, no-op for forwarding, graceful failure for fake IMAP), pipeline regression (extract + validate + learning growth + learned auto-match with confidence>=0.99), and cross-tenant isolation — pass on first run. The single security concern (missing HMAC verification on the WhatsApp webhook) is documented under critical_code_review_comments for the main agent to address before real production Meta credentials are wired in.
